parentPassport.name: ECZ-ID Business Passport™

tiers.name: Declared
tiers.description: Entry tier — establish your identity spine, no cost

tiers.name: Assured
tiers.description: Full assurance for regulated and high-stakes operations

parentPassport.description: The parent identity credential for any organisation entering the ECZ-ID ecosystem. Every child passport, package, and derived add-on attaches to this parent. Verified or Assured is required for child passport activation, badge provisioning, and package access.

derivedAddOn.name: ECZ-ID Insurability Readiness™
derivedAddOn.description: A derived add-on on the parent passport. It is NOT a package, NOT separately purchasable, and NOT manually editable. It is computed from active passports, evidence, and trust posture and indicates readiness for insurer engagement.
derivedAddOn.note: Included as a derived readiness signal on eligible parent passports. Not counted in the 25 packages.

families.name: Digital & Operational
families.description: Machine-readable identity for agents, cyber posture, APIs, AI models, datasets, IoT devices, and software supply chain systems.

families.name: Product, Risk & Transfer
families.description: Identity for products, custody transfer, and risk policy objects that need durable proof, provenance, and transfer integrity.

families.name: Robotics
families.description: Environment-based identity for robotic systems. Humanoids are handled as controlled variants within the correct robot passport — environment passport first, humanoid second.
families.humanoidNote: Humanoid is not a separate passport SKU. Use the correct environment passport first (Industrial, Public-Space, or Domestic), then apply the HUMANOID variant/profile. HUMANOID currently requires Assured parent tier.

families.name: Road & Freight Mobility
families.description: Identity for robotaxis, autonomous cars, autonomous haulage, cross-border freight, and high-value cargo mobility systems.

families.name: Aerial Mobility
families.description: Identity for drones and aerial systems across D1–D4 classes. Supports operator linkage, mission attestation, and mandate/insurance-grade aerial trust.
families.droneNote: D1, D2, D3, and D4 only. No extra drone passport families or unofficial aerial classes.

families.name: Infrastructure-Grade
families.description: Identity for intermodal transfer, industrial sites, critical infrastructure, funds flow, marine vessels, cargo containers, aircraft, and aviation components.
childPassports: ECZ-ID Financial Authority & Funds Flow Passport™

families.name: Control & Trust Overlays
families.description: Control-plane trust surfaces for platforms, continuity, and licensed operators that govern how the wider ECZ-ID system is relied upon.
